message:
fix(mcp_missions): give each save attempt a unique .tmp path to kill tmp-rename races

`mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path` and `mcp_save_mission_to_path`
both use the tmp-file + atomic-rename pattern:

  std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)?;
  std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, path)?;

so a mid-write crash leaves the original contents in place. The
tmp_path was being derived with

  let tmp_path = path.with_extension("json.tmp");

which is fine for a single writer but fatal when two concurrent
callers save the same contract at the same time: both writers race
on `tx-active.json.tmp`, one clobbers the other's JSON while the
other is mid-rename, and the rename step can reach a file whose
contents now belong to the other writer. We then atomic-rename a
partial JSON into the canonical path, producing a contract whose
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` refuses to parse — i.e.,
concurrent tx_run invocations could silently corrupt the tx
journal.

Fix: mint a per-attempt unique tmp path.

New private helper:

    fn unique_mcp_json_tmp_path(path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
        let nonce = MISSION_TMP_NONCE.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
        let pid   = std::process::id();
        let extension = path.extension()
            .and_then(|e| e.to_str()).unwrap_or("tmp");
        path.with_extension(format!("{extension}.{pid}.{nonce}.tmp"))
    }

backed by a file-scope
    static MISSION_TMP_NONCE: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);

so within a single process every tmp path is
`<path>.<pid>.<monotonic-nonce>.tmp`, and across processes the pid
component makes collisions between unrelated processes impossible.
`fetch_add(Relaxed)` is sufficient because the nonce only needs
uniqueness, not ordering.

Call sites:

- `mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path` (line ~362) now calls
  `unique_mcp_json_tmp_path(path)` instead of
  `path.with_extension("json.tmp")`.
- `mcp_save_mission_to_path` (line ~506) the same.

Both callers still use the resulting path in
`std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)` followed by
`std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, path)`, so the atomic-rename property
is preserved — every rename now targets the canonical path from a
writer-owned tmp file, with no cross-writer sharing.

Unused import `PathBuf` is pruned from `mod tests` — the test body
only needs `Path` now (the new helper returns a `PathBuf` but the
tests take references to it, and the one test site that constructs
a `PathBuf` uses the fully qualified path).

Regression test `tx_contract_tmp_paths_are_unique_per_save_attempt`:
- calls `unique_mcp_json_tmp_path(path)` twice on the same canonical
  path,
- asserts the two returned paths are distinct,
- asserts both live in the same parent directory as `path` (so the
  rename is still cross-filesystem-safe),
- asserts neither matches the legacy `path.with_extension("json.tmp")`
  — so accidentally reintroducing the shared tmp name is caught
  immediately.

Covers ft-bqfkr's race-atomic-rename vector directly; the
companion in-process advisory-lock fix for the same bead lives in
crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs.
